title: A technical specification label: black header bar, white body, engineering measurement marks. The `.precept` file IS a specification — it declares the exact structure of an entity the way a technical datasheet declares the tolerances of a manufactured part. The `->` operator in the central accent box is the key specification: the governed transformation that every instance must conform to.
conceptualBridge: The specification label means Precept's definition is a technical contract because the .precept file specifies exactly what an entity can do the same way a part datasheet specifies exactly what a component can withstand — both are enforced before any instance is produced.
decision: Rejected: 3.69 avg below threshold; legibility hard fail (2.5) — the spec label depends on text that is completely unreadable at 32px. The concept of 'Precept as technical specification' is worth revisiting with a more abstract/geometric execution that doesn't require text labels.
